application.doc: Retrieve the list of all active secrets for a specified API protection client

param.name: client_id
param.doc: The system-assigned API protection client ID value

method.name: GET
method.id: Retrieve the list of all active secrets for a specified API protection client
method.doc: To retrieve the list of all active secrets of an existing API protection client, issue an HTTP GET command on the client secrets resource URI specifying the ID of the API protection client.
Note: This API is only available for clients with hash secrets enabled.

request.doc: To determine the client_id value of the client to be updated, issue an HTTP GET command on the clients resource URI: https://{appliance_hostname}/iam/access/v8/clients/.

param.name: Accept:application/json
param.doc: Required for requests to the service.

response.doc: Bad Request. There is a problem with the request. The JSON result describes the problem.
Not Found. The requested service does not exist. The JSON message indicates which resource.
Conflict. The JSON result indicates the problem.
An internal error occurred. The JSON message indicates the problem.

representation.name: clientJsonSecret
representation.doc: Note: Response body is an array.

param.name: id
param.doc: A unique identifier to identify this client secret.

param.name: name
param.doc: A meaningful name to identify the new API protection client secret.

param.name: clientId
param.doc: A unique OAUTH client identifier to identify this client to the authorization server. It is sent in the token endpoint request to perform client authentication. If omitted, a random and unique alphanumeric string is generated and used as the client identifier.

param.name: created
param.doc: The milliseconds sinch epoch at which time this secret was created.

param.name: expiration
param.doc: The milliseconds sinch epoch at which time this secret will expire. If not set or set to 0 the secret will not expire.

param.name: lastUsed
param.doc: The milliseconds sinch epoch (truncated to the day of) at which time this secret was last used. If not set or the value is 0 or less means it has never been used.
